free_text: Gamma rays produced by the interaction of 15 MeV
neutrons in N, O, Mg and Al
An-400 HVE Van de Graaff accelerator.
Neutron energy of 15 MeV. The neutrons incident on
the large sample had an energy ranging between 14.7
and 15.5 MeV.
Ge(Li) detector, area of 70 mm2 and
thickness of 7 mm.
Resolution of detector system was about 7 keV at 1 MeV
and about 20 keV at 6 MeV.
Morgan et al.,Annual Prog.Rep. for period ending
Aug.1,1964,Texas Nuclear Corp.,and priv.comm.
Corrected for the variation of neutron flux in the
sample, neutron attenuation, multiple scattering
and gamma-ray attenuation.
The angle between the direction of the neutron and
that of the gamma-ray to the detector was within 50.
- 110. degr. Consequently the measured cross sections
are average values in this angular interval but are
referred to as 80. degr. differential cross sections.
No information except a remark: The counting
statistics were generally sufficient in order to
observe a gamma-ray line with a production cross
section exceeding about 1 mb/sr for gamma-ray energies
less than 3 MeV and about 0.5 mb/sr for gamma-rays
above 3 MeV
- The uncertainty in the gamma-ray energy is
estimated to be 3 keV below 3 MeV and 5 keV above
3 MeV, except for the Doppler-broadened lines in N
and O above 3 MeV, for which the uncertainty
is 10 keV.
SD:Updated to new date formats, lower case.
REACTION code was corrected in Subents 002-004,
006,007,010-018. Small corrections in all Subents.
COS -> ANG in Subents 002-019. BIB updated.
SD: Corrections in all Subents.
Liquid nitrogen contained in a thermos with a diam.
of 10.9 cm and a height of 25.6 cm served as
nitrogen sample
The gamma-ray energies have been corrected for
the Doppler shift.

The peak at 4.442 MeV results from the gamma-ray
transitions 4.439 -> 0. MeV in the l4N(n,t+gamma)l2C
reaction, and 4.444 -> to 0. MeV in the
14N(n,alpha+gamma)11B reaction.

The sample of natural magnesium and aluminium were
circular discs with diameters of 15.3 cm. Such
large samples were chosen to get reasonable yields
of high-energy gamma-rays (Eg > 1 MeV).
The gamma-ray energies have been corrected
for the Doppler shift.
